info.title: Sovereign Hybrid Compute User API
info.description:
Customer-facing VM API for authenticated SHC Blesta customers. Use it to read account data, compare storefront-backed VM plans, preview and submit a new VM order, inspect owned VM inventory, manage power, reinstall, backup, snapshot, and restore workflows, review invoices, and manage stored SSH keys. `GET /ordering/catalog` is the primary authenticated buy catalog, `POST /ordering/preview` validates a request, and `POST /ordering/submit` or `POST /vm/buy` creates the order through Blesta's canonical order engine. Backup, snapshot, and restore routes queue background jobs, and `GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs` or `GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{jobId}` exposes job progress. `GET /vm/templates` remains a descriptive plan and image endpoint for the customer's eligible VM scope and owned-service workflows. The `/user-api/v2` REST is a deprecated legacy transport, kept for backward compatibility; the flagship MCP (see the MCP note below) is the supported, go-forward agent surface. The wrapper handles authentication, rate limiting, routing, and response formatting, while an internal Blesta plugin model backs the business logic. Direct native `/api/UserApi.UserApiApi/...` access is intentionally disabled for customer-scoped methods. HTTP Basic uses your Blesta login username and password. For many accounts the username is the email address, but that is not guaranteed for every account type. If the account has 2FA enabled, send the current code in the `X-User-Api-OTP` header. Missing credentials return `401 Authentication required`. Missing OTP returns `401 Two-factor code required` and sets `X-User-Api-OTP-Required: 1`. Invalid password and invalid OTP also return `401` with endpoint-specific error messages. JSON request bodies are capped at 16 KiB. VM IDs in this API are Blesta service IDs, not Proxmox VMIDs. MCP note: this API exposes account, catalog, order, invoice, service, storage, and job state. It does not provide ambient screen or browser context by itself. The eligible authenticated, non-identity operations (118 MCP tools; identity, credential-management, and registration operations are excluded) are exposed as MCP tools at the flagship server https://mcp.sovereignhybridcompute.com/ (Model Context Protocol over Streamable HTTP), the preferred agent transport, with single-use confirm-token gating on every spend and destructive operation; authenticate with your shc_live_ operate key as a Bearer token. v1.8.0 documents the previously-undocumented Tier-2 write endpoints: VM shutdown/reset, ISO mount/unmount, firewall rule/policy management, live SSH key inject/remove, backup/snapshot verify acknowledgement, data-preferences update, support ticket create/reply/close, account preferences and credit-handling updates, contact deletion, and manager / managed-account management. Reinstall now returns 202 with a pollable job_id. v1.10.0 adds the Tier-2 service upgrade / package-change endpoints: GET /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade-options (read-only catalog of same-group plans + settable config options), POST /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade/preview (read-only prorated quote), and PATCH /vm/{serviceId}/upgrade (the money-moving change). The PATCH is QUEUED-ONLY: it always creates a prorated invoice and a queued change and returns 202; the VM is resized by Blesta's standard cron after the invoice is paid. pricing_ref is the raw package_pricing id. A disk-reducing change is always rejected (422) because the module would destroy and recreate the VM. The PATCH requires a body idempotency_key. v1.12.0 documents two shipped features: native AREA-scoped customer API keys and the enriched account activity report. Customer API keys (`Authorization: Bearer shc_live_...`, created/listed/revoked at /account/api-keys with Basic+OTP) now carry TWO ANDed authorization axes - a METHOD scope (read / operate / full) and an OPTIONAL area subset of Blesta's native contact-permission areas; each operation declares its governing area via `x-required-area`, a key created with no `areas` is materialized to the account's full permitted-area set (never stored unrestricted), and a key created with an `areas` array may only reach operations whose area is in the set (see the customerApiKey security scheme). GET /account/activity now merges sign-ins, failed sign-ins, account contact-detail changes, account-setting changes, and payment/transaction activity into one most-recent-first paginated feed (ActivityItem); it never exposes internal placement, staff identity, or the old/new values of changed fields. v1.14.0 expands the orderable OS/desktop catalog: Linux templates devuan5-cloud (Devuan 5) and openbsd79-cloud (OpenBSD 7.9), desktop environments kde (KDE Plasma) and mate (MATE) for AlmaLinux 9, and windows2022-byol (Windows Server 2022, Bring Your Own License). windows2022-byol is a dedicated Windows product with a 50 GB disk / 2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM minimum (the order form clamps to these), a BYOL acknowledgment, and standard linear compute pricing; it ships as Microsoft's 180-day evaluation and the customer applies their own Windows Server 2022 license (slmgr /ipk then /ato). os_template and gui_choice remain free-form strings resolved against the customer's eligible plan. v1.15.0 brings the documented OS/desktop catalog fully in line with the live, DB-driven /ordering/catalog: the orderable `template` option is debian13-cloud (default), debian12-cloud, ubuntu2404-cloud, ubuntu2204-cloud, fedora43-cloud, arch-cloud, nixos-cloud, almalinux9-cloud, alpine323-cloud, devuan5-cloud, openbsd79-cloud, and windows2022-byol; the `gui_choice` desktop option (AlmaLinux 9) is none/gnome/kde/xfce/cinnamon/mate. The catalog is authoritative and auto-reflects, so always read GET /ordering/catalog for the exact live set rather than relying on this prose. Additional images (ubuntu2604-cloud, freebsd14-cloud, openwrt-cloud, the NixOS 26.05 refresh, and Windows Server Core / Windows 11 desktop variants) are staged and will appear in the catalog as they are released.

## What's new in 2.4.0 (2026-07-02, non-breaking)

**Fixed**
- Missing-VM lookups on `GET /vm/{serviceId}/detail` and `POST /vm/{serviceId}/ssh-keys/apply-live` now return **404** with `error_code: not_found` (previously 500).
- `GET /vm/{serviceId}/summary` includes the live `runtime` object (state, lock, cpu_percent, mem_percent) — no second call to `/detail` needed for power state. If the hypervisor read fails, `runtime` is `null` and the summary still returns.
- All timestamps are now true UTC. Date fields such as `date_created` / `date_renews` were previously emitted 5 hours ahead; formats are unchanged (ISO-8601, `+00:00`).
- `GET /vm/{serviceId}/activity` now shows a VM's own provisioning tasks (freshly created VMs no longer return an empty feed).
- Orders that cannot resolve an OS template are rejected up front with **422** `template_required`, and the message lists the package's valid template values. `config_options` with option ids that belong to a different package are rejected with **422** and the valid option ids are listed.
- API responses now begin with `{` — the stray plaintext banner line is gone. You can remove any strip-prefix workaround.

**Added**
- `error.error_code` on every error — a stable machine code (registry below) so clients no longer match on message text. Transient conditions also carry `error.retry_after_seconds`.
- 409 confirmation challenges mirror the id flat at `confirmation.confirmation_id` (all nested copies kept).
- Field aliases (dual-emitted, both accepted where written): `balances`/`credit` on the two balance endpoints, `items` alongside `upgradable` on upgrade-options, singular `service_id` on the order response, `id` (+ `snapshot_id` on snapshots) alongside `backup_id` on snapshot/backup items, `pricing_id` alongside `pricing_ref` on upgrades. Snapshot/backup write bodies accept `backup_id` | `snapshot_id` | `id` | `volid`.
- Cancel responses include top-level `expected_refund` and `transaction_id` (see the refund formula below).
- Firewall `action` is case-insensitive on input (always UPPERCASE in responses). `GET /ssh-key` accepts `?service_id=`. `POST /vm/{serviceId}/console/session` accepts an optional `ttl` (seconds, clamped 5-300; default 30).
- `POST /agent-keys/claim` is documented: exchange a single-use claim code for an agent API key.
- MCP: `structuredContent.result` on every tool result (the unwrapped payload — uniform for reads and writes; the nested `data` shape is kept), a `listApiKeys` tool (full-scope keys may list key METADATA; creating and revoking keys stays Basic-auth-only), legacy tool-name aliases, and a `serverInfo.catalog` stamp (`spec_version`, `tool_count`).

### Machine error-code registry (`error.error_code`)

| error_code | Meaning | Retry |
|---|---|---|
| `not_found` | The addressed resource does not exist or is not owned by this account (404). | no |
| `invalid_token` | The API key was rejected (expired / revoked / unknown) — rotate or re-mint it. Missing credentials stay plain `unauthorized`. | after re-auth |
| `vm_locked` | The VM is transiently locked by another operation (for example a running backup). | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` (default 15) |
| `insufficient_credit` | The requested amount exceeds the available balance (currently: payout requests; VM orders never hard-fail on credit — they invoice). | after top-up |
| `upstream_failure` | The hypervisor / storage backend could not service the request. | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` (default 30) |
| `rate_limited` | Too many requests (429). | yes — honor `retry_after_seconds` |

Every other specific `error.code` value is mirrored into `error_code` verbatim. Sample error body:

```json
{"error": {"code": "conflict", "message": "Cancellation failed: ... VM is locked (backup) ...",
  "request_id": "1f0c...", "details": [], "error_code": "vm_locked", "retry_after_seconds": 15}}
```

### Cancellation refund formula

`expected_refund` / `cancel_credit.amount` come from ONE shared credit model (identical to the customer portal): for each paid invoice line covering the current term, credit = the line's cent-rounded amount x (unused seconds / covered seconds), summed in integer cents. A minimum charge of max(the term's 6-minute value, $0.01) is retained, already-issued this-term downgrade credits are subtracted, and the result is clamped to [0, amount paid]. End-of-term cancellations yield 0 (the term is fully used). The credit is issued as in-house account credit; `transaction_id` references it. Sample (1-day $0.24 term, canceled minutes after provisioning):

```json
{"data": {"id": 817, "service_status": "canceled", "expected_refund": 0.23, "transaction_id": 454,
  "cancel_credit": {"amount": 0.23, "currency": "USD", "transaction_id": 454, "issued": true}}}
```


## Request-body contracts & the MCP argument convention
Every operation that accepts a request body documents its exact fields (with a realistic example) in this spec's requestBody schema. How those fields map onto an MCP tool call (path/query params at the top level; the whole body under a single `body` object; the one `Idempotency-Key` header exception) is stated in the root `x-mcp-argument-convention` extension and in the docs page at `/user-api/docs/mcp-argument-convention.md`. Contracts are additive-only within a minor version; integrators may pin them. See `/user-api/CHANGELOG.md`.

mcp.note: The flagship MCP server exposes the eligible authenticated, non-identity operations (118 MCP tools; identity, credential-management, and registration operations are excluded) as MCP tools with single-use confirm-token gating on every spend and destructive op. Authenticate with your customer shc_live_ operate key (Bearer). The `/user-api/v2` REST base in `servers` is a deprecated legacy transport (kept for backward compatibility); prefer this MCP for new agent integrations.

x-agent-native.proprioception: Mutating responses carry a `next` object naming the single follow-up call that confirms the action: async producers (reinstall, backup/snapshot create+restore) return `next.poll` = /vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{job_id}; power verbs (start/stop/restart/shutdown/reset) return `expected_runtime_status` + `next.verify` = /vm/{serviceId}/summary; queued package-changes return `next.checkout_url`.
x-agent-native.error_contract: Errors follow a stable envelope {error:{code,message,request_id,details[]}}. Each detail MAY carry an optional stable `code` (required|invalid|already_in_use|out_of_range| unknown_field|unsupported_value) and a human/agent `hint`. On 429 the body MAY carry `error.retry_after_seconds` (mirrors the Retry-After header); errors MAY advertise `error.links[]` ({rel,href}) with rel in retry|status|docs|help|about.
x-agent-native.identifier_casing: operationIds are camelCase; resource identifiers in request/response BODIES are snake_case. Both styles are stable; do not transform one into the other.

operate_lane.summary: Obtain a SERVICE-SCOPED, cannot-spend operate Bearer from a customer Nostr grant (v2.0.1), no password shared.
operate_lane.request: Authorization: Nostr <base64 kind:27235 request>; body {grant: <customer-signed kind:30078 for your agent pubkey + one VM>}
operate_lane.result: A customer_key_operate Bearer scoped to the one granted VM; destructive (data-loss) and spend changes are confirm-gated (reinstall, restore/delete a backup or snapshot, cancel, delete a firewall rule, delete an SSH key, delete a contact/manager, ...) -- routine reads and non-destructive changes (power on/off/restart/shutdown/reset, ISO mount/unmount, firewall-rule add/edit, rDNS set, backup/snapshot create, add an SSH key, change backup schedule/notifications) are not. This mirrors the live customer portal (strict portal parity).

get.summary: List reverse-DNS-eligible IPs for a VM
get.description: Returns each IP assigned to this service that sits inside an SHC-managed reverse zone, with its current PTR, any pending (queued) value, the reverse zone, and a pending_public flag. Read-only mirror of the client portal Reverse DNS tab.

200.description: Reverse-DNS-eligible IPs for an owned VM.

pending_public.description: True when the reverse block is authoritative on SHC nameservers but not yet publicly delegated (e.g. Kansas 204.92.66).

post.summary: Set or replace the PTR for an owned IP
post.description: Sets the reverse-DNS (PTR) record for one IP assigned to this service. Ownership-gated (the IP must be on this VM and inside a managed reverse zone) and FCrDNS-gated (the hostname must already A/AAAA-resolve back to the IP). Applied asynchronously by a privileged worker, so this returns 202 with a job id; poll GET to watch it go live.

ip.description: An IP assigned to this VM, inside a managed reverse zone.

hostname.description: FQDN that already A/AAAA-resolves back to ip (FCrDNS).

409.description: A reverse DNS update is already in progress for this service.

error.message: A reverse DNS update is already in progress for this service.

422.description: Validation failed: ip not on this service / not in a managed zone, hostname not a valid FQDN, or FCrDNS not satisfied (hostname does not resolve back to ip).

delete.summary: Clear the PTR for an owned IP
delete.description: Clears the reverse-DNS (PTR) record for one owned IP. Applied asynchronously (202 with a job id).

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description: The legacy ?confirm=true query flag is NOT accepted (the v2-native dispatcher rejects it). Destructive/spend actions use a server-issued, single-use confirmation_id two-step. First call this operation WITHOUT a confirmation_id: it returns 409 confirmation_required carrying a non-secret confirmation_id (bound server-side to this exact call + your caller fingerprint, single-use, short-TTL) plus a how_to_proceed re-call shape. After a human has approved the data loss, re-send the IDENTICAL request adding header X-User-Api-Confirm: <that confirmation_id> to execute. The confirmation_id carries no secret -- it only names an already-bound pending action, so a leaked or parroted id is inert (it can only complete the identical call by the same principal). A confirmation_id is NOT permission -- only re-send it after the human's explicit yes to THIS action. Power actions (start/stop/restart/shutdown/reset) are not destructive and need no confirmation.

409.description: A reverse DNS update is already in progress for this service.

422.description: Validation failed: ip not on this service or not in a managed reverse zone.

get.description: Returns the authenticated customer's owned VM services. Use this to build service pickers, dashboards, or management views after a service already exists.

200.description: Canonical paginated list of Proxmox-backed VM services visible to the customer.

get.summary: List buyable VM plans for the authenticated customer
get.description: Returns a canonical paginated list of storefront-backed orderable VM plans visible to the authenticated customer. Each item includes pricing, embedded image metadata, and available order paths. Orderable OS templates currently include debian13-cloud (default), debian12-cloud, ubuntu2404-cloud, ubuntu2204-cloud, fedora43-cloud, arch-cloud, nixos-cloud, almalinux9-cloud, alpine323-cloud, devuan5-cloud, openbsd79-cloud, and the dedicated windows2022-byol (Bring Your Own License) product. The Operating System is the `template` configurable option and the optional Desktop Environment is the `gui_choice` option (none [default], gnome, kde, xfce, cinnamon, mate) on AlmaLinux 9; both appear under `config_options` in each plan with their full allowed value sets.

200.description: Canonical paginated list of buyable ordering plans for the authenticated customer.

post.description: Validates package selection, billing row, hostname, and optional location choice against the authenticated customer's visible storefront scope. This does not create the order or reserve capacity.

post.description: Creates a VM order and invoice through Blesta's canonical order engine. Provisioning still depends on the created order being accepted and its invoice being paid. The response echoes the effective `Idempotency-Key`. Reusing the same key with the same canonical request body within 24 hours replays the original `201` response; reusing it with a different body returns `409 idempotency_key_in_use`. A VM order whose resolved OS template is empty (no `template` config option and no package default) is rejected at submission with 400 `template_required` before any pending service or invoice is created -- supply the `template` config option to resolve this.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -sS -X POST https://blesta.sovereignhybridcompute.com/user-api/v2/vm/buy \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHC_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Idempotency-Key: $(uuidgen)" \
  -d '{"pricing_id": 42, "hostname": "my-vps", "config_options": {"template": "debian13-cloud"}}'
# 201 -> data.next.checkout_url; pay it, then poll data.id summary.

get.summary: List available OS templates and plans
get.description: Returns a canonical paginated list of descriptive VM plans for the authenticated customer's eligible VM scope. Each plan item embeds its default image metadata when available. Use `GET /image` for a template-only list.

200.description: Canonical paginated list of descriptive VM plans.

get.description: Returns the purchased package, current specs, assigned IPs, billing cadence, and renewal dates for one owned service. Use this after a service exists to inspect exactly what the customer has.

200.description: Detailed VM information for an owned service.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -sS -X PATCH https://blesta.sovereignhybridcompute.com/user-api/v2/vm/353/start \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHC_API_KEY"
# 200 -> {data:{expected_runtime_status:"running", next:{verify:"/user-api/v2/vm/353/summary"}}}
# Then GET data.next.verify until runtime_status == expected_runtime_status.

patch.description: Destructive operation. The VM must already be stopped, and the template name must be one returned by GET /vm/templates. The reinstall is asynchronous: it returns 202 with a job_id you can poll via GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{jobId}.

x-codeSamples.source:

parameters.description: The legacy ?confirm=true query flag is NOT accepted (the v2-native dispatcher rejects it). Destructive/spend actions use a server-issued, single-use confirmation_id two-step. First call this operation WITHOUT a confirmation_id: it returns 409 confirmation_required carrying a non-secret confirmation_id (bound server-side to this exact call + your caller fingerprint, single-use, short-TTL) plus a how_to_proceed re-call shape. After a human has approved the data loss, re-send the IDENTICAL request adding header X-User-Api-Confirm: <that confirmation_id> to execute. The confirmation_id carries no secret -- it only names an already-bound pending action, so a leaked or parroted id is inert (it can only complete the identical call by the same principal). A confirmation_id is NOT permission -- only re-send it after the human's explicit yes to THIS action. Power actions (start/stop/restart/shutdown/reset) are not destructive and need no confirmation.

get.description: Account-wide invoice list for the authenticated client. The `status` filter mirrors the client portal tabs and is a COMPUTED filter (not the raw invoice status column): `open` = not closed and billed; `closed` = closed and billed; `past_due` = past the due date and not closed. Readable with a read-, operate-, or full-scope customer API key whose permitted areas include this billing area (service-scoped keys are not eligible), or with Basic auth + OTP. Internal service-to-service bearer tokens are denied on every `/invoices/*` route.

get.description: Downloads the authenticated client's own invoice as a PDF document. The invoice is rendered server-side (TCPDF) and streamed as a binary `application/pdf` attachment — this is a binary download, **not** the standard JSON `{ "data": ... }` envelope returned by the other endpoints. Ownership is enforced at the SQL layer (`WHERE i.id = ? AND i.client_id = ?`) before any byte is emitted. Reachable with a read-, operate-, or full-scope customer API key whose permitted areas include billing (service-scoped keys are not eligible), or with HTTP Basic (plus `X-User-Api-OTP` when 2FA is enabled). Internal service-to-service bearer tokens are denied on every `/invoices/*` route (403/401 per the bearer audience gate). This endpoint has its own per-client+invoice+IP rate-limit bucket to bound the server-side render cost independent of the global request cap.

200.description: Invoice PDF (binary). Streamed as a `Content-Disposition: attachment` download, not the JSON envelope.

get.description: Reports whether the noVNC console feature is available for one owned VM service, mirroring the portal's console tab gate (node console enabled, console-eligible hostname, and active VM). This is a pure availability probe: it mints no console session and returns no VNC/PVE credentials, tickets, ports, or websocket URLs. To open a live console, POST /vm/{serviceId}/console/session.

200.description: Console availability for the service.

post.description: Mints a single-use, short-TTL, VM-scoped noVNC console session for one owned, active VM whose node enables the console feature, and returns a ready-to-open console URL plus its expiry. STRICT PARITY with the portal console tab: it runs the same admin-side PVE flow (ephemeral per-VM PVE user limited to VM.Console, expiring in ~60s; vncproxy ticket) and routes through the same secure noVNC bridge. The URL carries a short-lived signed token in its fragment and is meant to be handed to a human to open in a browser. The response NEVER includes PVE admin credentials, the PVE auth ticket, the node name, or the underlying VM id.

requestBody.description: Optional JSON body. Send Content-Type: application/json when present.

ttl.description: v2.4.0 (additive): requested session ttl in seconds, clamped to [5, 300]. Absent = the deployed default (30s). Note the PVE-side VNC ticket has its own upstream validity; open the console promptly.

get.description: Returns Proxmox RRD time-series for CPU, RAM, disk read/write, and network in/out for one owned VM. Mirrors the client portal Manage tab charts (consolidation is fixed to MAX server-side). Each series is an array of [epoch_seconds, value] pairs; dividers convert raw values to display units (RAM bytes->GiB, disk/network bytes->MiB). A freshly provisioned VM may return empty value arrays. Data before the current provisioning generation (last_install) is filtered out.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: RRD window. Defaults to hour.

200.description: Time-series metrics for an owned VM.

get.description: Returns the full DB detail (same fields as GET /vm/{serviceId}, with the service lifecycle status surfaced as service_status) PLUS a live runtime snapshot (power state, lock, CPU/RAM percent), the network interface list, and a GPU/PCI summary card. get.description:
One agent-friendly call: the same ownership-checked, placement-hidden DB detail as GET /vm/{serviceId} (identity + service_status/provisioning_state + current period/renewal) PLUS a recent-jobs aggregate and a has_active_job flag, so "is it ready / is anything running?" needs one round-trip instead of several. DB-only (no live Proxmox call); use GET /vm/{serviceId}/detail for a live runtime snapshot.

v2.4.0: response now includes the live `runtime` object (nullable, tolerant).

get.description: Returns the stored transfer usage versus the package cap for one owned VM. used_bytes is the customer-visible synced figure (service_fields.bandwidth_usage) shown on the portal Manage tab, NOT a live RRD recomputation. as_of is the last bandwidth sync timestamp. Unit note: limit_gb uses the GB (/1000) convention consistent with GET /vm/{serviceId} specs.bandwidth_gb; the portal Manage card's TB label is a display-only quirk.

200.description: Bandwidth usage summary for an owned VM.

get.description: Lists the live Proxmox node task history for one owned VM (qmstart, qmstop, vzdump, etc.), scoped to the current provisioning generation. This is distinct from GET /vm/{serviceId}/jobs, which lists Blesta-side background jobs. Each item carries only command type, start/finish times, and status; infrastructure identifiers (UPID/node/PID/PVE user) are stripped. pagination.total is best-effort (Proxmox does not return a reliable count) — treat the X-User-Api-Has-More header as authoritative for whether another page exists. The VM is resized by Blesta's standard cron after the invoice is paid (it is never resized synchronously in this request). The request body MUST include idempotency_key (a missing key returns 400); reusing the same key with the same canonical body for the same service replays the original 202 response, and reusing it with a different body returns 409 idempotency_key_in_use. A disk-reducing change returns 422 (the module would destroy and recreate the VM). A change that requires no payment (zero or credit-only) returns 409 no_payable_change. Placement (node/host/storage/vmid) is never exposed.

post.summary: Add account credit (top up) via BTCPay
post.description: Mints a BTCPay payment with NO invoice attached, so the settled funds become unapplied **account credit** — the API equivalent of the customer portal's *Add Funds*. Returns a hosted `checkout_url` and, when available, a **BOLT11 Lightning invoice** (`bolt11`) and on-chain address so a wallet or automation can pay directly. Requires a full-scope customer API key or Basic+OTP; operate-scoped keys are rejected (this is a money-movement route, so an operate key cannot spend). The `amount` must be a positive decimal with at most 2 places (no rounding). Idempotent on the body `idempotency_key`. The credit lands once the BTCPay payment confirms (via webhook).

post.summary: Create a customer account and receive a usable API key in one anonymous call.
post.description: Anonymous, one-call customer signup. Creates a Blesta client account (the same client-creation path as the web order/registration form) and returns a pre-minted, scoped customer API key in the SAME 201 response, so a developer or AI agent goes from nothing to a usable account plus first API key without a second round-trip. No authentication is required (this is the only unauthenticated write endpoint documented here; the agent key-bootstrap /agent-keys/claim is the one other public route, an internal pairing endpoint). Email is the login identifier (no username). No CAPTCHA, no postal address, no phone, no email-verification gate. The new account is INERT until a first order is placed and its invoice is paid (registration provisions nothing and costs nothing). This endpoint is FRICTIONLESS and is NOT anti-enumeration: if the email is already registered it returns a plain 409 'email_exists' (this is intentional and accepted). The minted key cannot reach identity/credential routes regardless of scope (api-keys, password, 2FA, contact, and the primary-identity PATCH /account are Basic+OTP-only for every key). Rate limited per IP (global edge limit plus a tighter registration bucket). An optional Idempotency-Key header is honored at the rate-limit level only (full replay-storage is deferred for v1; a lost response is recovered by logging in to generate a key).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client-supplied idempotency key (16-128 chars). It is ACCEPTED for forward compatibility but has NO effect on /register in v1: registration is rate-limited by a per-IP bucket (5 per hour) that is ALWAYS consumed regardless of this header, and the key is deliberately NEVER folded into that bucket — if it were, rotating keys could mint a fresh slot each time and bypass the cap (this is intentional anti-bypass, not a limitation). The response is NOT stored or replayed, so reusing the key does NOT return the original 201; a duplicate email always returns 409 email_exists. Full byte-identical replay is deferred.

If the account was created but the API key could not be minted on this call, `api_key` is null and the account is recoverable via the portal/HTTP Basic (log in and generate a key); the call still returns 201 (it does not fail an already-created account).

post.description: Creates a Bitcoin payout REQUEST (status `pending`). This only records the request in Blesta — it does NOT send any funds; the actual disbursement is a separate operator process. Mirrors the client portal payout request: only one pending request is allowed at a time (`409 conflict` otherwise), the requested amount must not exceed the available balance, and it must fall within the affiliate's minimum/maximum withdrawal limits (`422 validation_failed` otherwise). Amounts are BTC-native (8 dp). `requested_currency` is optional and must equal the affiliate withdrawal currency (BTC). `payment_method_id` is optional; when only one payout method is configured it is selected automatically. Returns `404 not_found` if the client is not enrolled.

Each referral carries the (anonymized) order number, status, invoiced amount, and accrued commission in BTC. Strictly scoped to the client's own affiliate. Returns `404 not_found` if not enrolled.

Client-ownership scoped like every other /vm/{serviceId} endpoint — a foreign / unowned / non-VM serviceId returns 404.

post.summary: Rekey (rotate) a VM's zero-knowledge backup registration

Two-step confirm: first call without X-User-Api-Confirm to receive a 409 confirmation_id, then re-send the identical request with the header after a human approves. The body carries the client-derived recipient pubkeys + KDF config; SHC never receives the password or any private key.

post.summary: Claim a single-use agent API key by claim code
post.description: Public (unauthenticated) claim endpoint for operator-issued agent keys. The customer (or SHC on their behalf) mints a key and hands the AGENT only a single-use claim code; the agent exchanges the code here exactly once and receives the plaintext key (never recoverable again). Anti-enumeration: every existence-type miss (unknown / expired / already-claimed / raced) returns one uniform 404 body on a uniform response-time floor. Malformed requests are 422. Rate limited per source IP (30/10min) and per code (10/10min). NOT exposed as an MCP tool (public credential bootstrap; the MCP surface is authenticated).

basicAuth.description: Use your Blesta login username and password. For many accounts the username is the email address, but that is not guaranteed for every account type. If the account has 2FA enabled, also send X-User-Api-OTP with the current one-time code. Missing credentials return 401 Authentication required. Missing OTP returns 401 Two-factor code required.

internalToken.description: Internal SHC bearer token for AI bots and service-to-service calls. Tokens are audience-scoped and may be authorized for only a subset of audited operations. For example, the `contextvm_support_runtime` audience is limited to GET `/account`, GET `/vm/templates`, GET `/vm/{serviceId}`, GET `/vm/{serviceId}/backups`, GET `/vm/{serviceId}/snapshots`, GET `/vm/{serviceId}/jobs`, and GET `/vm/{serviceId}/jobs/{jobId}`. This internal-token scheme is for staff/system callers only. Customers do NOT use it; a customer authenticates with HTTP Basic (+OTP) or with a customer-minted API key (the separate customerApiKey scheme, Authorization: Bearer shc_live_...). Successful and failed internal-token requests are audit logged.

customerApiKey.description: A customer-managed scoped API key: Authorization: Bearer shc_live_... . Create/list/revoke at /account/api-keys (Basic+OTP only). Two independent authorization axes are ANDed (both must pass): (1) METHOD/scope: read (GET only), operate (no money/billing/credential ops), full (all except credential management); (2) AREA: an OPTIONAL subset of Blesta native contact-permission areas. Each operation declares its governing area via x-required-area. When 'areas' is omitted (the default) the key is NOT stored unrestricted: it is materialized to an explicit CSV of the account's own permitted areas (for the account owner that is every alias below), so it reaches all areas you can access while never carrying a NULL/all-areas scope. A key created with an 'areas' array may only reach operations whose x-required-area is in that set. Area aliases: client_invoices, client_quotations, client_services, client_transactions, client_contacts, client_accounts, client_emails, _credits, _managed, support. Identity/credential routes (x-required-area=__identity__: /account/api-keys*, /account/password, /account/2fa*, /account/contact, PATCH /account) are NEVER reachable by ANY key (Basic+OTP only), regardless of scope or areas. Area scope never substitutes for tenant isolation: client_id ownership is always enforced server-side.

config_options.description: Optional package configurable options / add-ons to apply to the order, as a map of package-option ID (a positive integer, sent as the string key) to the selected value. The available option IDs, labels, and allowed values for each plan are listed under `config_options` in GET /ordering/catalog. Example: selecting the +1 TB disk add-on whose option ID is 15 → {"15": "1024"}. Omit to take the plan defaults. The Operating System and Desktop Environment are themselves configurable options: the `template` option (label "Operating System") selects the OS image (e.g. debian13-cloud, debian12-cloud, ubuntu2404-cloud, ubuntu2204-cloud, fedora43-cloud, arch-cloud, nixos-cloud, almalinux9-cloud, alpine323-cloud, devuan5-cloud, openbsd79-cloud, windows2022-byol), and the `gui_choice` option (label "Desktop Environment", AlmaLinux 9) selects an optional desktop (none, gnome, kde, xfce, cinnamon, mate). Their option IDs and allowed values are listed under `config_options` in GET /ordering/catalog. IMPORTANT: a VM order requires a resolved operating-system template. If the selected package does not define a default OS template, you MUST include the `template` option in config_options (use the option ID and an allowed value from GET /ordering/catalog); otherwise the order is rejected at submission with 400 error code `template_required` (no pending service/invoice is created). If the package defines a default OS template, `template` is optional and the default is used.

fingerprint.description: Present when a matching key was removed.

message.description: Present for idempotent no-op deletes when the fingerprint is not stored.

VmSummaryList.description: Canonical paginated list of customer VM summaries.

PlanList.description: Canonical paginated list of descriptive VM plans. Each item embeds its default image metadata when available.

OrderablePlanList.description: Canonical paginated list of orderable VM plans.

TemplateList.description: Canonical paginated list of VM templates/images.

StorageItemList.description: Canonical paginated list of backup or snapshot items.

ProxmoxJobList.description: Canonical paginated list of background jobs.

InvoiceSummaryList.description: Canonical paginated list of invoice summaries.

InvoiceLineList.description: Canonical paginated list of invoice line items.

SshKeyEntryList.description: Canonical paginated list of stored SSH keys.

AppliedPayment.description: A transaction applied to an invoice (shown when inv_display_payments is enabled).

AccountInvoiceDetail.description: Customer-safe single invoice with line items and optional applied payments.

applied_payments.description: Present only when the inv_display_payments company setting is enabled.

credit.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): per-currency available credit as {currency, amount} — the same list /account/balance calls 'credit'.

EmailSummary.description: Customer-safe email / notice list row (internal columns stripped).

body_preview.description: First ~280 characters of the plain-text body.

EmailDetail.description: Customer-safe email / notice detail with full body.

VmIso.description: ISO images and CD-ROM state for an owned VM.

mounted.description: CD-ROM drives currently mounted on the VM.

volid.description: Proxmox volume id of the mounted ISO.

available.description: ISO images available to mount (excludes those already mounted).

size_bytes.description: ISO size in bytes, if reported.

boot_order.description: Current boot order (read-only; changed only via mount/unmount).

VmFirewall.description: Per-VM firewall configuration and rule-form vocabulary.

policy_in.description: Default inbound policy (ACCEPT, DROP, or REJECT).

policy_out.description: Default outbound policy (ACCEPT, DROP, or REJECT).

interfaces.description: VM network interface keys (e.g. net0) selectable on a rule.

items.description: v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

icmp_types.description: Supported ICMP types for ICMP rules.

VmFirewallRule.description: A single firewall rule. Field names mirror the Proxmox rule object as consumed by the client portal; 'type' is the rule direction (in/out).

pos.description: Rule position (ordering key). Positions renumber when a rule is deleted.

enable.description: 1 if the rule is enabled, 0 otherwise.

VmMetricSeries.description: One RRD metric series. values is an array of [epoch_seconds, value] pairs. divider (when present) converts raw values to display units.

prefixItems.description: Sample value (raw; apply divider for display units). CPU is a 0-1 fraction.

divider.description: Divide raw values by this for display units (RAM 1073741824=GiB; disk/network 1048576=MiB). Absent for CPU.

rate.description: Shaping rate in Mbit, or null when unshaped.

VmRuntimeOverview.description: Live power-state snapshot (subset of the Proxmox status/current; host-identifying fields are omitted).

state.description: raw_status, or 'locked' when a lock is held.

lock.description: Lock reason when locked (e.g. backup, migrate, snapshot).

VmPciSummary.description: GPU/PCI passthrough summary card. Only the device count and a primary label/short are exposed; per-device topology (pci_id, vendor:device, IOMMU group) is intentionally withheld.

primary.description: Null when no PCI devices are assigned.

VmActivityItem.description: One Proxmox task. Infrastructure identifiers (UPID/node/PID/PVE user) are stripped for tenant isolation.

status.description: PVE task exit status ('OK' on success), or 'running' when not yet finished.

PermissionOptionsEnvelope.description: Vocabulary of permission areas that may be granted to a contact or account manager, as key/label pairs. Returned by GET /contacts/permission-options and GET /managers/permission-options (the manager vocabulary is the contact vocabulary minus the _managed area).

permissions.description: Grantable permission areas. Keys mirror the portal's permission options, including any plugin-contributed areas.

label.description: Human-readable label for the permission area.

QuotationSummary.description: Compact view of one quotation owned by the authenticated client. Money fields are formatted as fixed-point strings (two decimal places) to preserve precision. Returned directly in the GET /quotations list; the GET /quotations/{quotationId} detail response composes this summary with notes and line_items via allOf.

id_code.description: Formatted quotation number shown to the customer.

subtotal.description: Quotation subtotal before tax, as a fixed-point string with two decimal places.

total.description: Quotation grand total, as a fixed-point string with two decimal places.

currency.description: ISO-4217 currency code for the quotation amounts.

VmIsoMountRequest.description: Mount an ISO image as a new CD-ROM drive. The ISO must be one offered by GET /vm/{serviceId}/iso and not already mounted.

iso.description: Proxmox volume id of the ISO to mount (from the `available` list of GET /vm/{serviceId}/iso).

VmIsoUnmountRequest.description: Unmount a mounted ISO CD-ROM drive. Idempotent: an absent/already-unmounted drive returns 200 with the refreshed list.

drive.description: QEMU IDE cdrom drive key to detach (from the `mounted` list of GET /vm/{serviceId}/iso), e.g. ide2.

VmDataPreferencesUpdateRequest.description: Update backup/snapshot schedule, retention, notification, and encryption-pubkey preferences. PATCH semantics: only the sections/keys present are changed. At least one recognized field is expected.

backup.description: Backup or snapshot scheduling preferences. Only the keys present are updated (PATCH semantics).

retention.description: A configured retention preset key, the literal `keep-all`, or a comma-separated `keep-<unit>=<n>` policy (units: last, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, yearly).

auto_days.description: Days the automatic job runs (configured schedule-day keys).

auto_time.description: Hour the automatic job runs, in `HH:00` form.

snapshot.description: Backup or snapshot scheduling preferences. Only the keys present are updated (PATCH semantics).

retention.description: A configured retention preset key, the literal `keep-all`, or a comma-separated `keep-<unit>=<n>` policy (units: last, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, yearly).

auto_days.description: Days the automatic job runs (configured schedule-day keys).

auto_time.description: Hour the automatic job runs, in `HH:00` form.

notify.description: Notification toggles. Each accepts a JSON boolean or the strings on/off/true/false/1/0.

encryption_pubkey.description: Reserved — client-side backup encryption is not yet available; a non-empty value returns 501.

VmFirewallRuleCreateRequest.description: Create a firewall rule. `action` and `direction` are required; all other fields are optional. The raw Proxmox `type` key is rejected (use `direction`).

action.description: Rule action. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

direction.description: Rule direction. (Security-group rules cannot be created/edited via the API.)

source.description: Source address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name (comma-separated tokens allowed).

source_port.description: Source port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

dest.description: Destination address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name.

dest_port.description: Destination port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

protocol.description: Protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) or a numeric protocol id 0-255.

macro.description: A firewall macro name from GET /vm/{serviceId}/firewall, or `none` for no macro.

interface.description: Bind to a VM network interface name (e.g. net0).

icmp-type.description: ICMP type, from the firewall vocabulary (GET /vm/{serviceId}/firewall `icmp_types`).

enabled.description: Whether the rule is enabled. Accepts a JSON boolean or on/off/true/false/1/0. Defaults to true on create; on edit, omitting it keeps the current state.

VmFirewallRuleUpdateRequest.description: Edit a firewall rule by position. PATCH semantics: only the supplied fields change; at least one recognized field is required. `action`/`direction` are NOT required on an edit. The raw Proxmox `type` key is rejected.

action.description: Rule action. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

direction.description: Rule direction. (Security-group rules cannot be created/edited via the API.)

source.description: Source address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name (comma-separated tokens allowed).

source_port.description: Source port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

dest.description: Destination address: IP, CIDR, or an IPSet/alias name.

dest_port.description: Destination port: a port, a `lo:hi` range, a comma list, or a service name.

protocol.description: Protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) or a numeric protocol id 0-255.

macro.description: A firewall macro name from GET /vm/{serviceId}/firewall, or `none` for no macro.

interface.description: Bind to a VM network interface name (e.g. net0).

icmp-type.description: ICMP type, from the firewall vocabulary (GET /vm/{serviceId}/firewall `icmp_types`).

enabled.description: Whether the rule is enabled. Accepts a JSON boolean or on/off/true/false/1/0. Defaults to true on create; on edit, omitting it keeps the current state.

VmFirewallRuleListResponse.description: The refreshed client-visible firewall rule list after a rule mutation (security-group rules excluded).

VmFirewallPolicyUpdateRequest.description: Set the default inbound/outbound firewall policy. PATCH semantics: only the keys present are set; at least one of policy_in/policy_out is required.

policy_in.description: Default inbound policy. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

policy_out.description: Default outbound policy. v2.4.0: case-INSENSITIVE on input (accept/Drop/REJECT all valid); always canonical UPPERCASE on output.

VmFirewallPolicyResponse.description: The refreshed default firewall policy after an update.

VmSshKeyApplyLiveRequest.description: Live-inject a single-line SSH public key into the running VM via the guest agent. The key is also persisted so a later reinstall keeps it.

ssh_key.description: Single-line ssh-rsa, ssh-ed25519, or ecdsa-sha2-nistp256/384/521 public key.

VmSshKeyApplyLiveResponse.description: Result of a live SSH-key inject. The live guest-exec result is best-effort and not authoritatively confirmable, so `live_inject` is always `attempted` (never `success`).

key_fingerprint.description: Server-computed SHA-256 fingerprint of the injected key (pass to DELETE /vm/{serviceId}/ssh-keys/live to live-remove it).

VmSshKeyDeleteLiveRequest.description: Live-remove an SSH key from the running VM by fingerprint, and scrub it from the persisted set.

key_fingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the key to remove. `SHA256:` and `MD5:` forms are accepted.

VmSshKeyDeleteLiveResponse.description: Result of a live SSH-key removal. Idempotent: `removed` is false when the fingerprint was not present.

VmStorageVerifyRequest.description: Acknowledge a verify request for an owned backup/snapshot volume. The backup_id must exist in this VM's own inventory. v2.4.0: exactly one of backup_id | snapshot_id | id | volid is required (aliases, first present wins).

backup_id.description: Opaque, per-customer backup/restore-point handle (`bk_…`). Returned in place of the real storage volume id so the underlying Proxmox vmid/node is never disclosed. Use this value verbatim as the restore/delete/protection/verify/file-restore/restore-hints handle; it is mapped back to the real volume server-side.

snapshot_id.description: v2.4.0 alias of backup_id (accepted on input).

id.description: v2.4.0 alias of backup_id (accepted on input).

VmStorageVerifyResponse.description: Synchronous no-op verify acknowledgement (no background job is queued).

backup_id.description: Opaque, per-customer backup/restore-point handle (`bk_…`). Returned in place of the real storage volume id so the underlying Proxmox vmid/node is never disclosed. Use this value verbatim as the restore/delete/protection/verify/file-restore/restore-hints handle; it is mapped back to the real volume server-side.

SupportTicketAttachment.description: A base64-encoded file to attach to a ticket or reply.

SupportTicketCustomFields.description: Department custom-field values keyed by numeric field id. Values are scalar (string/number/boolean).

SupportTicketCreateRequest.description: Create a support ticket. `department_id`, `subject`, and `message` are required.

department_id.description: Target support department (must be visible to this client; see GET /support/departments).

subject.example: Cannot reach my VM over SSH

message.example: My VM stopped responding to SSH around 14:00 UTC.

priority.description: Priority key valid for the department. Defaults to the department's default priority.

attachments.description: Optional base64-encoded attachments (subject to a total-size cap).

example.subject: Cannot reach my VM over SSH
example.message: My VM stopped responding to SSH around 14:00 UTC.

SupportTicketReplyRequest.description: Add a reply to an owned ticket. Replying to a closed/awaiting-reply ticket reopens it.

CreditHandlingUpdateRequest.description: Full replacement of the per-currency low-credit notification threshold map. An empty `thresholds` object clears all thresholds.

thresholds.description: Map of ISO-4217 currency code to threshold amount. Each currency must exist for the company.

AccountPreferencesUpdateRequest.description: Update self-scoped, company-editable client preferences. PATCH semantics: only the keys present are changed; at least one field is required. Only fields the company has flagged editable (see GET /account/preferences `editable`) may be changed. `autodebit` is not writable through this API.

inv_address_to.description: Contact id to address invoices to (must be one of this client's contacts).

ManagedAccountInvitationRequest.description: Accept or decline a management invitation addressed to the authenticated client.

action.description: Whether to accept or decline the invitation.

ManagedAccountInvitationResponse.description: Result of accepting/declining a management invitation.

managed_client_id.description: The client id this client now manages (on accept) or that the invitation referenced.

already_resolved.description: Present and true when accept is a no-op because the invitation was already accepted (idempotent).

ManagerDeleteResponse.description: Result of revoking an active manager (returns contact_id) or declining a pending invitation (returns status=declined).

contact_id.description: Present when an active manager (numeric ref) was revoked.

status.description: Present (value `declined`) when a pending invitation (token ref) was cancelled.

ActivityItem.description: One customer-visible account activity event, merged most-recent-first from Blesta's native logs (sign-ins, failed sign-ins, account contact-detail changes, account-setting changes, and payment/transaction activity). Polymorphic on `type`: only the fields relevant to that event kind are populated; type-specific fields are null/absent for other types. NEVER exposes internal placement (node/VMID/host), staff identity, or the old/new VALUES of changed fields.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC time of the event; null if the source datetime was zero (0000-00-00 00:00:00).

timestamp_epoch.description: Unix epoch of the event (the sort key); null if the source datetime was zero.

ip_address.description: login / login_failed only: the customer's OWN source IP. Null for other event types.

changed_fields.description: contact_change / setting_change only: the NAMES of the changed fields (allow-listed). Field names only — never the previous/new values. Empty for other event types.

changed_count.description: contact_change/setting_change: the TOTAL number of changed fields (a count only). May exceed the length of changed_fields, which lists only the allow-listed, customer-safe field NAMES.

amount.description: transaction only: 2-decimal amount string. Null for other event types.

currency.description: transaction only: ISO-4217 currency code. Null for other event types.

status.description: transaction only: transaction status. Null for other event types.

AccountActivityList.description: Canonical paginated list of account activity events.

VmUpgradeConfigOptionCatalog.description: A settable/addable config option for an upgradable plan, identical in shape to the ordering catalog (option_id is the raw package option id).

VmUpgradeOptionTerm.description: One client-orderable term for an upgradable plan. pricing_ref is the raw package_pricing id to pass to preview/PATCH.

recurring_amount.description: Recurring price (base plan) at this term.

setup_fee.description: One-time setup fee at this term.

VmUpgradablePlan.description: A same-group package the service may move to. disk_reduces / disk_change_blocked flag a base-disk-shrinking move (rejected at commit). Placement is never present.

disk_gb.description: Base package disk (GB), config options excluded.

available_config_options.description: Catalog of settable config options per term (ordering catalog shape).

disk_reduces.description: True if a no-add-on move to this plan would reduce disk.

disk_change_blocked.description: True if a disk-reducing move is rejected at commit.

VmUpgradeOptionsResponse.description: The current plan + the same-group plans the customer may move to, with a blocked_reason when a change cannot proceed. Placement is never exposed.

disk_gb.description: Current effective disk (base meta + current disk options).

blocked_reason.description: Why a change cannot proceed right now, or null.

items.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): identical to 'upgradable' (the generic list key).

VmUpgradePreviewRequest.description: Target pricing_ref (raw package_pricing.id) + optional config_options to price. v2.4.0: one of pricing_ref | pricing_id is required (aliases; pricing_ref wins if both sent).

pricing_ref.description: Raw package_pricing.id (same-group, client-allowable, same billing term).

config_options.description: Map of package option id (string) -> selected value, validated identically to ordering.

pricing_id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): synonym of pricing_ref. pricing_ref wins if both are sent.

VmUpgradePreviewResponse.description: Prorated quote (no charge). amount_due_now already includes any setup fee. applies is always "queued" (the change is created queued, awaiting payment).

prorate_credit.description: Prorated credit for a downgrade, only when account credit is enabled (else 0.00).

amount_due_now.description: Amount due now (includes setup fee). 0.00 when nothing is owed.

new_recurring_amount.description: New recurring amount = base renewal + config-option recurring totals.

setup_fee_total.description: Discrete setup-fee subtotal, only when the presenter exposes it (already in amount_due_now).

VmUpgradeRequest.description: Target pricing_ref + optional config_options + the REQUIRED idempotency_key. v2.4.0: one of pricing_ref | pricing_id is required (aliases; pricing_ref wins if both sent).

pricing_ref.description: Raw package_pricing.id (same-group, client-allowable, same billing term).

config_options.description: Map of package option id (string) -> selected value (validated identically to ordering).

idempotency_key.description: REQUIRED. Service-scoped idempotency key (a missing key returns 400). Reuse the same value with the same body to replay the original 202 for this service.

pricing_id.description: v2.4.0 alias (additive): synonym of pricing_ref (the RAW package_pricing.id, the same id the ordering catalog calls pricing_id). pricing_ref wins if both are sent.

VmUpgradeResponse.description: Result of an accepted (queued) package change. change is always "queued"; the prorated invoice_id is awaiting payment and the change is applied by cron once that invoice is paid.

amount.description: Amount due on the prorated invoice.

new_recurring_amount.description: New recurring amount (base + ALL merged config-option recurring).

amount.description: Amount of account credit to add. Send a positive decimal STRING with at most 2 decimal places (e.g. "25.00"); a bare integer is also accepted. A JSON float is REJECTED (422) so the customer is charged EXACTLY what they type — no silent rounding. Must be > 0 and within the account's configured credit limits.

currency.description: Optional ISO-4217 code (defaults to the client's currency). Must be an active company currency.

return_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL BTCPay redirects the browser to after payment. Non-https or non-allowlisted hosts are rejected with 400.

idempotency_key.description: REQUIRED client-supplied idempotency key (body field). Reuse the same value with the same body to replay the original response instead of minting a second top-up invoice.

amount.description: The credited amount as a 2-decimal string.

checkout_url.description: BTCPay hosted checkout page (pay by Lightning or on-chain in a browser).

bolt11.description: A BOLT11 Lightning invoice for the amount, for wallets/automation that pay without the hosted page. Null if a direct Lightning invoice is unavailable for this invoice.

onchain_address.description: On-chain Bitcoin address, when the store offers on-chain. Null on a Lightning-only store.

payment_link.description: A single wallet-openable payment URI (prefers lightning:lnbc…, else lightning:lnurl…, else bitcoin:…).

email.description: The account login identifier (used as the HTTP Basic username). Must be a valid email address.

password.description: Account password. Minimum 8 characters; no other complexity rule is imposed.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (case-insensitive; stored uppercased). Optional; defaults to US. Validated against the supported country list.

recovery_email.description: Optional alternate email for password-reset delivery.

tos_accepted.description: REQUIRED. Must be true: the caller affirms the end user accepted SHC's Terms of Service (https://blesta.sovereignhybridcompute.com/tos). A missing, null, or false value is rejected 400 tos_required. Mirrors the web registration form's terms gate.

scope.description: Optional scope for the API key minted at registration. Defaults to 'operate' (read + provision, never spend). The anonymous /register endpoint is capped to {read, operate}; full keys are issued only via your account settings (authenticated POST /account/api-keys) after you sign in. Note that no scope can reach identity/credential routes (api-keys, password, 2FA, contact, primary-identity PATCH /account) — those are Basic+OTP-only for every key.

RegisterApiKey.description: A customer API key minted at registration. The plaintext `key` is shown ONCE.

key.description: The secret API key (Bearer credential), shown once and never retrievable again. Begins with 'shc_live_'.

key_prefix.description: Non-secret display prefix (first 12 characters of the key).

scope.description: Key scope (the requested 'scope', defaulting to 'operate'). The /register endpoint mints only read or operate keys; no scope can reach identity/credential routes.

expires_at.description: Key expiry timestamp (90 days after creation).

api_key.description: The minted API key, or null if the account was created but the key could not be minted on this call (recover by logging in and generating a key).

next.description: Pointers to the next steps (catalog browse, docs).

note.example: Account is inert until a first order is placed and its invoice is paid. Authenticate with the api_key Bearer token, or HTTP Basic (email + password).

commission_amount.description: For percentage, the percent (e.g. "2"); for fixed, a BTC amount.

cookie_days.description: Referral attribution cookie window, in days.

maturity_days.description: Days before a referral commission matures.

available.description: Available to withdraw now, BTC (8 dp) = total_available - total_withdrawn, floored at 0.

AffiliateAccount.description: Affiliate account overview. When `enrolled` is false only `enrolled`, `status` (= "not_enrolled"), `eligible`, and `program` are present.

eligible.description: Only present when not enrolled: whether the active-service gate is met.

payout_onchain.description: Bitcoin on-chain destination (xpub/ypub/zpub or address), or null.

payout_lightning.description: Lightning destination (Lightning Address, LNURL, or BOLT11), or null.

AffiliatePayoutDestinationUpdate.description: At least one field required. Empty string clears that destination; omitted leaves it unchanged.

payout_onchain.description: Extended public key (xpub/ypub/zpub, testnet variants) or a single mainnet address; "" to clear.

payout_lightning.description: Lightning Address, LNURL, or BOLT11 invoice; "" to clear.

requested_amount.description: Amount to withdraw, BTC. Must be > 0, <= available balance, and within min/max withdrawal limits.

requested_currency.description: Optional; must equal the affiliate withdrawal currency (BTC).

payment_method_id.description: Optional payout method id; defaults to the single configured method.

NextPoll.description: Poll pointer for an async (queued-job) producer.

poll.description: Job-status endpoint to poll until the job reaches a terminal status.

NextVerify.description: Verify pointer for a fire-and-confirm action (power verbs).

verify.description: Authoritative re-read endpoint (the VM summary, which also carries has_active_job).

NextCheckout.description: Checkout pointer for a payment-gated, queued change (package upgrade).

note.example: The package change is queued; it is applied automatically once the prorated invoice is paid. A disk size increase takes effect after you reboot the VM.

ConsoleSessionResponse.description: A freshly minted, single-use noVNC console session. Open console_url in a browser before it expires.

console_url.description: Ready-to-open noVNC URL with a short-lived signed token in the fragment. Single-use.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 (UTC) instant when the session token expires.

ttl.description: Session ttl in seconds (v2.4.0: request a custom ttl via the optional body, clamp 5-300).

ContactCreateRequest.description: Fields for creating a client-owned contact. Mirrors the portal add-contact form.

contact_type.description: Non-primary contact type: 'billing' (default), 'other', or a numeric custom contact-type id (stored as 'other').

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Defaults to the account's own country if omitted.

phone.description: Optional single phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15125550123).

permissions.description: Permission area keys to grant this contact. Unknown keys are ignored. See GET /contacts/permission-options.

enable_login.description: Create a portal login for this contact. When true, new_password is required (and confirm_password must match if supplied).

username.description: Login username when enable_login is true (defaults to the email). Only valid with enable_login.

new_password.description: Password for the contact's portal login. Required when enable_login is true; only valid with enable_login. Must satisfy the company password policy.

confirm_password.description: Must equal new_password. Defaults to new_password if omitted. Only valid with enable_login.

ManagerInviteRequest.description: Fields for inviting an account manager. Mirrors the portal add-manager form.

permissions.description: Permission area keys to grant the manager (non-empty). Unknown keys are ignored; see GET /managers/permission-options.

status.description: 'pending' = invitation email sent to an existing same-company account; 'invalid' = no matching account, recorded without an email (stock Blesta behavior).

ZkBackupRegistration.description: Zero-knowledge backup registration: client-derived X25519 pubkeys + immutable KDF config. Exactly one recipient must be kind=password (the primary). The server never sees the password or private keys.

config.description: Immutable per-service KDF config; salt is 16 client-random bytes (lowercase hex).

kind.description: How this recipient's X25519 public key was derived — what the customer holds to unlock a restore (five-rung recovery model; any one recipient suffices): `password` — a passphrase (argon2id-derived key). `recovery-key` — a generated high-entropy recovery code the customer stored. `passkey` — a FIDO2 passkey using the PRF extension. `shamir` — one share-set of a split secret (threshold recovery). `ssh-ed25519` — an existing Ed25519 SSH key (converted). `secp256k1` — a secp256k1 key such as a Nostr identity (the input alias `nostr` is accepted and normalized to `secp256k1`). `pgp` — an OpenPGP key. `btc` — a Bitcoin key. `other` — any other X25519-capable material the customer manages.

pubkey.description: X25519 public key (hex); private key never leaves the client.

XRequestId.description: Correlation UUID for the request. If the client supplies a valid `X-Request-Id` request header, the API echoes it back; otherwise the API generates one.

XUserApiTotalCount.description: Total matching items for the collection represented in the response body. On `GET /payment/{invoiceId}` this applies to the nested `line_items` collection.

XUserApiLimit.description: Page size used for the collection response body.

XUserApiOffset.description: Starting offset used for the collection response body.

XUserApiHasMore.description: Whether another page exists after the current response body.

XUserApiNextOffset.description: Offset value to request the next page when `X-User-Api-Has-More` is `1`.

IdempotencyKey.description: Effective idempotency key for this order submission, echoed from the request or generated by the server.

x-mcp-argument-convention.summary: How an MCP tool's arguments map onto this REST API's request. Every MCP tool inputSchema is generated 1:1 from this OpenAPI document by McpCatalog::buildInputSchema; pin against this document and this convention.
x-mcp-argument-convention.top_level_arguments: Each path and query parameter becomes a TOP-LEVEL tool argument, named VERBATIM as it appears in this spec. Path parameters are camelCase (serviceId, ticketId, pos) and are always required.
x-mcp-argument-convention.request_body: The ENTIRE requestBody is passed as a single nested object argument named 'body'. Its fields are exactly the requestBody schema fields in this spec, in snake_case (ssh_key, policy_in, backup_id, department_id, ...). When requestBody.required is true, 'body' is a required argument.
x-mcp-argument-convention.header_exception: HTTP headers are NOT tool arguments, with ONE exception: Idempotency-Key becomes a top-level argument literally named 'Idempotency-Key' (buyVirtualMachine / createVirtualMachineOrder).
x-mcp-argument-convention.strictness: The tool inputSchema is additionalProperties:false and performs NO snake_case<->camelCase coercion. An unknown TOP-LEVEL key is rejected by the MCP binder ('Unexpected argument supplied'); an unknown BODY field is rejected by the REST handler ("Unknown field '<x>'"). Put path/query args at the top level and ALL request-body fields under 'body'.
x-mcp-argument-convention.id_placement: Deleting or modifying a sub-resource by id is modeled two ways in this API and both are intentional (see the per-operation x-shc-id-placement extension): firewall RULES take the id as a PATH parameter (pos) with no body; backups/snapshots take the id as a BODY field (backup_id, with snapshot_id/id accepted as aliases).
x-mcp-argument-convention.idempotency_placement: Idempotency is a HEADER (Idempotency-Key) on the order ops and a BODY field (idempotency_key) on submitPaymentCheckout and submitCreditTopup. See each operation.
x-mcp-argument-convention.read_vs_write_asymmetry: reverse-DNS: the READ (GET /vm/{serviceId}/rdns) returns 'ptr'; the WRITE (POST) body field is 'hostname'. Use 'hostname' on write.
